title: Instruments of a Beating Heart
subTitle: First graders in a Tokyo public elementary school are presented with a challenge for the final semester. Examining the Japanese educational system's tenuous balance between self-sacrifice and human growth as it teaches the next generation.

title: Instruments of a Beating Heart
subTitle: First graders in a Tokyo public elementary school are presented with a challenge for the final semester. Examining the Japanese educational system's tenuous balance between self-sacrifice and human growth as it teaches the next generation.

title: Mitateru-Phose: Let's Change Perspective!
subTitle: Using *mitateru*, a Japanese method, to stimulate children's imaginative thinking by prompting them to perceive objects in unconventional ways, fostering their individual viewpoints and inventiveness.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Cycle Around Japan
subTitle: Exploring a side of Japan best discovered by bicycle, including fascinating places that regular tourists never get to see and that some locals don't even know exist.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Time and Tide
subTitle: Highlights a variety of historical events with a keen focus on the ties between Japan and other countries, and explores complex worldwide movements from the perspective of Japanese history.

title: The Seasons of Yamato
subTitle: A series of poetic videos by Koichi Hozan, a videographer living in Nara, in collaboration with a pianist, Mine Kawakami, for which she plays exclusive pieces. This time, Keichitsu.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: Summer is winding down, and tonight down by Edo Bay the streets are filled with professional entertainers, food sellers, and revelers. It's getting late, but this party is just getting started.

title: Grand Sumo Highlights
subTitle: Sumo highlights from a range of tournaments that have taken place across the globe. Sumo is one of the most traditional Japanese sports and boasts a history going back more than 1500 years.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Sumopedia enriches the sumo experience with information on the techniques, traditions and famous wrestlers of the past. The rules may be simple but there is a lot more to sumo than meets the eye.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Japan Delish
subTitle: Curry rice, one of Japan's most iconic national dishes. Why is this dish, born in India, so beloved by the Japanese?

title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life
subTitle: We look at Self-Defence Forces disaster prep, including their response team's special equipment and techniques, and a new tsunami prediction system.

title: Design Museum Japan
subTitle: In a coastal district, boat builders used their skills to make houses. Ryue Nishizawa explores how architecture is affected by a community's terrain and values.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Fact-Checking America
subTitle: As claims of fake news spread, PolitiFact,one of the most respected fact-checkers in the U.S., examines statements coming out of Washington, offering an inside look at journalism in search of truth.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Let's Trek Japan
subTitle: Japan's snowscapes transform into a winter wonderland every winter, where climbing snowy peaks provide a thrilling but risky adventure. Following along in Kita-Yatsugatake for safe mountain fun.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
subTitle: Born yesterday has a very different meaning for Mandarin ducklings in the big city. Their second day of life is a rite-of-passage, avoiding crows and man-made hazards in search of the nearest pond.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Shimizu Daisuke investigates various aspects of Japanese culture and traditions using the technique of time-lapse photography.

title: Design Ah! neo
subTitle: As different colours, blue and yellow can make green by getting together, similarly different lives can coexist by helping each other.

title: Design Museum Japan
subTitle: In a coastal district, boat builders used their skills to make houses. Ryue Nishizawa explores how architecture is affected by a community's terrain and values.

title: I Love 'Mee'
subTitle: Seeing the baby laugh when tickled, Mee thinks everyone likes tickles and happily tickles every child in sight. But Mee's friend Hee suddenly gets angry and says, Stop it! Don't tickle me!

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: J-MELO
subTitle: May J showcases a selection of anisong fan favourites and welcomes MYTH & ROID to the studio for a chat and a live performance.

title: Little Charo
subTitle: The adventures of Charo, a Japanese dog who finds himself lost in New York City and decides to find his way back home.

title: Japanology Plus Mini
subTitle: Explores the growing shift towards a circular economy through the professional repair of electronic circuitry and the creative upcycling of discarded garments.

title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
subTitle: Kuro explores the rugged Tojinbo Cliffs in Fukui and heads over to the nearby shopping street. He finds Quu in a lacquerware workshop, who makes her owner's legs fall asleep by sitting on his lap.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Somewhere Street
subTitle: A unique walking-eye view of the greatest cities around the globe. Featuring chats with locals and enjoying encounters that only strolling the city streets can bring.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Okinawa Prefecture is a group of subtropical islands in the far south of Japan. We examine its past and present, with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: Homes.

title: Grand Sumo Highlights
subTitle: Sumo highlights from a range of tournaments that have taken place across the globe. Sumo is one of the most traditional Japanese sports and boasts a history going back more than 1500 years.

title: Sumopedia
subTitle: Gives a peek into an envelope containing the prize money that the sumo wrestler will receive after winning the match.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Ramen Japan
subTitle: Explores Yamagata's status as Japan's ramen capital, featuring fiery Akayu-style miso topped with a signature spicy paste and delicate Sakata shoyu ramen crafted with handmade noodles and seafood broth.

title: Core Kyoto Mini
subTitle: A look at the timeless heart of Japan's ancient capital. Against its rich backdrop of culture and tradition, today's Kyoto continues to innovate and inspire, even after being established 1,200 years ago.

title: Easy Japanese for Work
subTitle: Ngoc-san, from Vietnam, wants to help a new coworker sitting next to him and become a great senior, Senpai. To know and understand the proper way, Ngoc-san will tackle a role-play.

title: Zero Waste Life
subTitle: Gardener Yamashita Rikito collects plants with nowhere to go, that must be removed for demolition or renovation, and finds them new homes; saving them while saving those who give them up from regret.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: On the Edge
subTitle: Exclusive footage takes you inside Russian-occupied areas of Ukraine. As residents are compelled to change citizenship, and even identity, they face stark choices: to adapt, remain silent, or resist.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Herbal Symphony
subTitle: In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Anime Manga Explosion
subTitle: Discovers the secrets of Japanese anime and manga as their popularity continues to grow. Considers the appeal of these Japanese productions internationally, how they're created, and their cultural significance.

title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
subTitle: Rockie and friends go into Clattery Woods and help a stranger who claims to be a cactologist move his stuff before learning about his research.

title: Texico
subTitle: Explores how people can improve their analysing skills using visualisation, which makes tasks easy to follow.

title: Chatroom Japan
subTitle: How would you reach out to the person next to you in times of disaster? That is the question Amy Takebe asks her students based on her experience in 2011.

title: IMPACTS:Climates Change the World
subTitle: Each year, 20 million people are displaced by climate change. If nothing is done, these climate refugees will number 216 million by 2050. We examine footage of problem areas and the latest research.

title: Design Museum Japan
subTitle: In a coastal district, boat builders used their skills to make houses. Ryue Nishizawa explores how architecture is affected by a community's terrain and values.

title: A Mile in Your Shoes
subTitle: We look at the efforts of Israeli-American robotics expert Tamir Blum, who develops AI robots that will help Japanese agriculture deal with issues such as labor shortages and the aging population.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo Mini
subTitle: Focusing on moyashi, also known as bean sprouts. They may not look like much, but they're packed with vitamins and minerals. They're easy to use, and most importantly, affordable, lending to their popularity.

title: NHK Newsline
subTitle: Hourly news updates featuring current affairs, business news, financial updates and global weather forecasts.

title: Time-Lapse Japan
subTitle: Masses of clay figurines called dogu have been recovered from sites in Hokkaido, Tohoku, Kanto and Chubu from the middle, late and final Jomon periods. The Sannai Maruyama village site alone turned up 1,500 figurines.

title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life
subTitle: We look at Self-Defence Forces disaster prep, including their response team's special equipment and techniques, and a new tsunami prediction system.

title: Activate Your Japanese
subTitle: Lessons on how the viewer can make the most out of their Japanese language skills. This time, a look at what one can say and do when it is time to change the subject in a social environment.

title: Culture Crossroads
subTitle: Culture Crossroads comprises several regular programs with the theme of creating a multicultural society. They look at international exchange programs and give tips on disaster preparedness.

title: Hello! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
subTitle: March 2026 marks 15 years since the Great East Japan Earthquake. Throughout the month, NHK World-Japan will show programs looking at the present and future of the disaster-hit areas.
